skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 250+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Access 250+ AI models through inference.sh CLI for image generation, video creation, LLM calls, and web search
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 203 potential issues, primarily pattern detection. After semantic evaluation: backtick/shell commands are the legitimate CLI wrapper mechanism, not injection vulnerabilities. Hardcoded URLs are documentation examples. The skill combines code execution, network access, and credential handling to call external AI services - this is documented functionality, not malicious behavior. No evidence of data exfiltration or malicious intent. Risk factors are inherent to CLI wrapper operations.

medium_findings.title: CLI Wrapper Inherently Combines Elevated-Risk Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill legitimately combines code execution, network access, and credential handling to call external AI services via inference.sh. This is the documented purpose. No malicious exfiltration or unauthorized access detected.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Heuristic flagged this pattern but semantic analysis confirms these are legitimate CLI wrapper operations, not malicious code execution

low_findings.title: MD5 References in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged MD5 usage as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These appear in documentation examples showing how to compute hashes, not in security-critical code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 in documentation examples is not a vulnerability - these are showing usage patterns, not implementing security features

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs. These are API endpoint documentation examples, not actual runtime network calls to untrusted destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation URLs showing available endpoints is standard practice and not a security risk

content.user_title: Run 250+ AI Models via inference.sh CLI
content.value_statement: Need to generate images, create videos, or call AI models? This skill wraps the inference.sh CLI to give Claude access to 250+ AI services including FLUX, Veo, Gemini, and Claude itself.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Seedance, and other diffusion models
Create videos with Veo, OmniHuman, and similar AI video tools
Call LLMs including Claude, Gemini, Grok, and OpenRouter models
Perform web searches via Tavily and Exa search APIs
Automate Twitter posts and interactions through Twitter API
Access 3D rendering and other specialized AI applications
limitations: Requires valid inference.sh API key configured in environment
Rate limits and quotas depend on third-party AI service providers
Output quality depends on upstream AI model capabilities and availability

use_cases.title: Generate Marketing Images
use_cases.description: Create product images, social media graphics, or marketing assets using FLUX or Seedance models directly from Claude
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams needing quick AI-generated visuals

use_cases.title: Access Claude via External CLI
use_cases.description: Use Claude models through the inference.sh wrapper for batch processing or scripted workflows
use_cases.target_user: Developers automating LLM interactions in pipelines

use_cases.title: Research Web Search
use_cases.description: Query web search services like Tavily or Exa to gather research information for analysis
use_cases.target_user: Researchers and analysts gathering real-time data

prompt_templates.title: Generate an image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use inference.sh to generate an image of [description] using FLUX model

prompt_templates.title: Create video content
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the following video generation using inference.sh with Veo: [scene description]

prompt_templates.title: Call LLM for analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Using inference.sh, send the following text to Gemini for sentiment analysis: [text]
prompt_templates.scenario: LLM API call for text processing

prompt_templates.title: Web search for research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web using inference.sh with Tavily for information about [topic]

output_examples.input: Generate a landscape image of a mountain at sunset using FLUX
output_examples.output: Image generation initiated via inference.sh... Download URL provided upon completion

output_examples.input: Call Gemini to summarize: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
output_examples.output: Summary: A concise description of a fox's agility demonstrated through a common pangram

best_practices: Set INFERENCE_API_KEY in environment before using skill features
Check rate limits before running batch operations on AI services
Verify output from AI models before using in production systems
anti_patterns: Do not pass untrusted user input directly as inference.sh arguments without validation
Do not expose API keys in skill configurations or shared prompts
Do not rely solely on AI-generated content without human review

faq.question: How do I get an inference.sh API key?
faq.answer: Sign up at inference.sh to obtain an API key. Set it as INFERENCE_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Which AI models are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports 250+ models including FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, OpenRouter, Tavily, and Exa.

faq.question: Are there rate limits?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your inference.sh plan and the specific AI service being accessed.

faq.question: Can I use this to call Claude?
faq.answer: Yes, you can access Claude through inference.sh. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and use the Claude model option.

faq.question: Is image generation free?
faq.answer: Image generation costs depend on the model provider. Check inference.sh pricing for details.

faq.question: Can I automate Twitter?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports Twitter API integration. Configure your Twitter API credentials properly.
